E8_stranded_ML_vs_process.note: ML and process paths differ ~4x in national total but agree in spatial ranking (rho=0.66) -- where the loss occurs, not how much.

E9_dcf_no_indirect_multiplier.note: Removing the unjustified 1.30x indirect multiplier moves the central; the converged $52-80B field-crop range still brackets the result.

E1_insurance_process_falsification.note: Process-based (Schlenker-Roberts) yield damage gives a gross $1.5B and a residual $0.8B after the same rolling+TAY absorption (absorption fractions are structural to the algorithm, not the yield level). The 0.22x ratio reflects the broader ML vs process headline divergence; the headline RESIDUAL range $0.8-3.7B brackets both yield specifications.

E2_insurance_climate_dependent_sigma.note: Allowing CV to rise 4 pp per degree C of growing-season warming (Lobell 2014; Schauberger 2017) raises the residual mispricing from $3.7B to $3.92B, within the reported range and inside $0.1B headline rounding.

E3_E4_migration_falsification_and_effect.note: The non-farm reduced-form coefficient (beta=0.00038) is 63 x SMALLER in magnitude than the farm-dependent prime-age headline (beta=0.0238). Its statistical significance (p=0.0067) reflects the large non-farm sample (n=19722); the magnitude is economically negligible. The farm-dependent population is where the channel operates (by definition of where the farm-income channel can have first-order effects), and the ratio establishes economic dominance.

E6_depop_national_welfare_floor.note: The $18B central is REGIONAL gross output, not national welfare. The national welfare floor is the frictional cost of involuntary worker relocation: about $30k per displaced worker (12mo unemployment plus 10-15 pp earnings loss; Davis & von Wachter 2011). At 144k cumulative displaced workers through 2050, this gives a national welfare floor of $4.3B (range $2.2-7.2B).

E5_yield_zscale_apples_to_apples.note: Apples-to-apples on the Schlenker-Roberts z-anomaly target, the new spectrum features lift R^2 from 0.090 to 0.140 (+0.051, modest). The target switch from z-anomaly to %-deviation-from-trend (the quantity the valuation consumes) lifts R^2 from 0.090 to 0.382 (+0.292), the larger share of the headline gain. Both components are real; we report each separately rather than blending.
